source.title: Hippocampal place-cell sequences depict future paths to remembered goals

confidence_rationale: Landmark empirical paper. Direct electrophysiological recording in rats demonstrating that theta-cycle-compressed place-cell sequences sweep forward from current position to remembered goal, predicting immediate future behaviour in novel start-goal combinations. Mechanistic link from theta oscillation to sequential trajectory representation is explicit and strongly supported by the data. Transfer risk: rodent place cells are the substrate, and whether equivalent theta-compressed sequences exist across abstract conceptual dimensions in E1 remains undemonstrated.
failure_signatures: Sequences occurred specifically before and during navigation, not during idle rest — suggesting they are prospective planning signals, not mere replay.
Sequences were biased toward goal location even for novel start-goal combinations, ruling out simple route replay and implicating generative traversal of the associative manifold.
Sequence events were confined within individual theta cycles (~125 ms), consistent with theta as the temporal compression mechanism.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Before goal-directed navigation, rat hippocampus generates brief theta-cycle-compressed sequences encoding spatial trajectories from current location to remembered goal, even for novel start-goal combinations, predicting immediate future movement.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-156 asserts that theta oscillations implement sequential traversal across indexed E1 representations. The Pfeiffer & Foster data are the canonical demonstration: within each theta cycle, place cells activate in a compressed sequence that constitutes a prospective sweep through the associative manifold. The rat is not replaying a stored route — it is generating a novel trajectory by traversing the indexed map. This is exactly the substrate MECH-156 points to: theta as the clock that serialises associative lookups into ordered sequences suitable for planning.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The substrate is rodent CA1 place cells in two-dimensional spatial navigation. MECH-156 makes a broader claim about traversal across E1's abstract indexed representations (not limited to space). Whether the theta-sweep mechanism generalises from spatial to non-spatial associative manifolds requires separate evidence (e.g., the Behrens 2018 review provides theoretical grounding). The translation is plausible but the direct demonstration of non-spatial theta sequences of this quality does not yet exist in the empirical record.
mapping.source_context: Rodent (rat), CA1 hippocampus, two-dimensional open arena, goal-directed navigation with multiple known goal locations, tetrode recordings, LFP theta reference

confidence_components.notes: Source quality near ceiling: Nature publication, landmark finding, widely replicated in direction if not exact detail. Mapping fidelity high because the mechanistic claim (theta cycle = unit of sequential traversal) maps directly onto MECH-156. Transfer risk moderate: spatial-to-abstract generalisation is the main gap, partially addressed by grid-cell/abstract-space literature but not yet demonstrated for theta sequences specifically.
